hypothesis: Replacing the static empirical valency lookup table with a dynamic, graph neural network-based valency predictor trained on the corrected GEOM-Drugs dataset—one that conditions valid valency ranges on the full local chemical environment of each atom rather than only on element type, formal charge, and aromatic bond count—will yield a molecular stability metric that better discriminates between chemically plausible and implausible generated structures, resulting in a stronger correlation with GFN2-xTB relaxation energies than the best lookup-table-based metric proposed in this paper.
